name: Barry COVID-19 Helping Hands
description:
Welcome to Helping Hands! We are a group of community spirited volunteers who want to be neighbourly and support the people of Barry.

If you are shielding, self-isolating or need support for another please get in touch. We can do shopping, pick up prescriptions, dog walking, posting mail, befriending and welfare calls. If you need something else, have a confidential chat with one of our coordinators and we will see what we can do to help.

Contact us 9.00-8.00pm, 7 days a week.

Coddington Volunteers
description:
This volunteer service has arisen from the current COVID-19 health crisis. There is a need for some members of the community who may need to self-isolate to receive help from others, be it in the form of help with shopping, IT support, posting letters and/or packages, or needing someone to talk to.

Equally, there's a requirement for a recipient of any help to know that any volunteer is bona fide. Hence all volunteers have to be recommended by another member of the community, given that news articles are already circulating of thieves and scammers presenting themselves as volunteers elsewhere in the country.

The purpose of this service is to provide a single view of community members known to need help and set them in contact with those able to provide that help, so that volunteering is not done in a piecemeal, sporadic fashion wherein some people may fall through the cracks, but is instead done consistently. That is the aim.

name: One Bedfordshire
description:
One Bedfordshire has been set up to support our local community during the COVID-19 outbreak.  We do this through the volunteers who can support the local people of Bedfordshire who are self-isolating or those who are at most risk from the virus to stay well.

Volunteers must be 18 or over, and fit and well with no symptoms. Your safety is our priority. The majority of tasks can be undertaken while social distancing and volunteers receive guidance on staying safe and have undertake DBS checks.

You are able to pick any of the following support services.

Community Collection Services: Volunteers collect shopping, medication or other essential supplies for you who are self-isolating, and delivering these supplies to your home.

Check-in and Chat Services: Volunteers provide short-term telephone support to you who are at risk of loneliness as a consequence of self-isolation.

Community First Aid: Volunteers are individual who has undertaken appropriate training . They must hold a valid certificate of competence in either: first aid at work, emergency first aid at work or any other level of training or qualification that is appropriate.
